title: Hemionitis fraseri
intro: Hemionitis fraseri, also known as the Fraser's golden-fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Hemionitis fraseri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in humans.
taxonomy: Hemionitis fraseri is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of evergreen fern native to the southeastern United States. The genus Hemionitis is part of the fern order Polypodiales. Hemionitis fraseri is also known as Fraser's hemionitis or Fraser's leather fern. Within the kingdom Plantae, Hemionitis fraseri is classified as a vascular plant and a flowering plant is not applicable as ferns reproduce via spores.
